content:
Have you ever taken medicine to feel better when you're sick? Sometimes, we take antibiotics to fight off bad germs that make us feel yucky. But, if we take antibiotics too much or when we don't really need them, the germs can get stronger. It's like they develop a special power to fight off the medicine. This is called antibiotic resistance.

When we overuse antibiotics, the good germs in our body get killed, and the bad germs get stronger. It's like a superhero battle, and the bad germs are winning. That's why it's essential to only take antibiotics when we really need them. We can also help by washing our hands often to prevent the spread of germs. If we're careful, we can keep the bad germs from getting too strong, and the medicine will keep working to make us feel better.
